title: satureja cuneifolia subsp. obovata
intro: Satureja cuneifolia subsp. obovata, also known as mountain savory, is a subspecies of the Satureja cuneifolia plant, a member of the Lamiaceae family. This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of mountain savory include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Mountain savory can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
